challenges.title: Greeting Function
challenges.description: Write a function `greet(name, greeting="Hello")` that returns a formatted greeting string using an f-string: `f"{greeting}, {name}!"`.
challenges.starter_code:
def greet(name, greeting="Hello"):
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test your function
print(greet("Alice"))
print(greet("Bob", "Hi"))
challenges.test_code:
assert greet("Alice") == "Hello, Alice!"
assert greet("Bob", "Hi") == "Hi, Bob!"

challenges.title: Flexible Average
challenges.description: Write a function `average(*args)` that accepts any number of numeric arguments and returns their average. If no arguments are provided, return 0.
challenges.starter_code:
def average(*args):
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test your function
print(average(1, 2, 3))
print(average())
print(average(10))
challenges.test_code:
assert average(1, 2, 3) == 2.0
assert average() == 0
assert average(10) == 10.0

challenges.title: Config Builder
challenges.description: Write a function `build_config(**kwargs)` that returns a dictionary containing all keyword arguments passed to it, plus default values of `debug=False` and `verbose=False` for any keys not explicitly provided.
challenges.starter_code:
def build_config(**kwargs):
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test your function
print(build_config())
print(build_config(debug=True, name="test"))
challenges.test_code:
assert build_config() == {"debug": False, "verbose": False}
assert build_config(debug=True, name="test") == {"debug": True, "verbose": False, "name": "test"}
